game_name: Anno 1602: Creation of a New World

design_philosophy.core_loop: Discover islands → Colonize → Build economy → Satisfy population needs → Unlock buildings/trade routes → Expand to new islands → Repeat with increasing complexity
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing economic growth and resource production chains against population demands and potential riots/decline
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: European colonial empire builder in the Age of Exploration; creating a thriving, self-sustaining economic network across an archipelago
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Explicitly designed to reward peaceful economic strategies over military conquest; intentionally nationalistically neutral (no civ differences); technology serves colony development rather than warfare dominance; emphasizes simulation and player agency in world-building rather than scripted progression

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Progressive adaptive AI that adjusts pace based on player speed; player can choose between scenario-based campaigns or free-form play; multiple viable strategies (peaceful vs military) allow difficulty self-calibration

progression.upgrade_system: Buildings must be technologically upgraded throughout game to satisfy increasingly demanding citizen classes (Pioneers → Settlers → Citizens → Merchants); upgrades unlock new building types and production capabilities
progression.unlock_mechanism: Population milestones gate access to new buildings (e.g., must reach certain population before accessing weapons factories); citizen class advancement unlocks building types and economic chains
replayability_hooks: Random map generation for infinite free-play variety
User-made custom scenarios via Scenario Builder
Multiple win conditions (economic dominance, military conquest, or hybrid)
16 total scenarios with progressive difficulty curve introducing new mechanics

world_building_techniques: Historical setting (1602 AD Age of Exploration)
Archipelago discovery and island settlement structure
Emergent narrative through colony evolution and citizen advancement
Neutral faction interactions (native tribes, free traders)
Economic chains and production networks create implicit narrative of civilization development

faction_indicators: Player nations represented by colored banners (intentionally generic, no civ asymmetry)
CPU-controlled neutral factions (native tribes, free traders)
Multiplayer opponents in competitive economic/military scenarios
danger_signals: Population riots from unmet citizen demands
Economic collapse from broken production chains
Military invasion from computer or human opponents
Resource depletion on islands limiting expansion
environmental_storytelling: Colony housing evolves architecturally as citizens advance social classes
Island development reflects player's economic priorities and settlement patterns
Production chains visualized through interconnected buildings and trade routes
Archipelago layout creates natural geographical narrative of exploration and discovery
